description:
Creality Print is a self-developed Fused deposition modeling slicing software produced by Creality.

It's a practical and easy-to-use tool that can help you remote control and monitor your 3D printer and help print directly from your computer.
New Features
1.  Added support for Creality WiFi Box-related features and printers connected via WiFi Box.
2.  Improved file saving efficiency when using "Save Project As".
3.  Optimized the automatic model layout function.
4.  Improved the Copy & Paste (Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V) experience — pasted models will no longer overlap.
5.  Enhanced the Clone (Ctrl+K) operation with a preview effect when cloning models.
6.  Optimized model placement upon import — models are now automatically arranged (this behavior can be disabled in Preferences).
7.  Added a new "Layout Selected" feature to the Auto Layout function — only the selected models will be arranged within the build plate.
8.  Improved behavior during single-plate layout — when models do not fit within the plate, they will no longer "disappear" but instead be placed above Plate 1.
9.  Enhanced the Fill Build Plate feature — added a secondary menu allowing adjustments such as model spacing.
10. Introduced a Grid Layout option within the Fill Build Plate feature — models are forcibly arranged in a tidy checkerboard pattern, with a visual preview.
11. Enhanced the file sending page with additional information, including real-time transfer speed display.
Bug fixes
1.  Fixed an issue where the wipe tower was incorrectly displayed in single-color mode and mismatched in the object list and build plate. Thanks to @三月的狮子 for the suggestion.
2.  Fixed a boundary overflow issue with the wipe tower on Creality Hi model. Thanks to @Tanarnat Thai and Dr. Subashi Samarasinghe for the suggestion.
3.  Fixed shadow artifacts in the G-code preview page after applying Z-axis compensation to side-curved models. Thanks to @张勇 for the suggestion.
4.  Fixed an issue where both “Default” and “default” groups appeared simultaneously in the device list.
5.  Fixed a bug where logging out could occur automatically after restarting the software on macOS. Thanks to @梓申 for the suggestion.
6.  Fixed an issue where a recolored single-color model appeared with two colors when sending to the printer.
7.  Fixed an issue where Fluuid devices with the default name "other" could not be renamed. Thanks to @LookAtTheShinyShiny for the suggestion.
8.  Fixed a bug where STL files dragged into the software did not display model names.
9.  Fixed an issue where models in some 3MF files overlapped after auto layout.
10. Fixed a bug preventing PLY files from being opened via drag-and-drop or double-click.
11. Fixed several UI issues on macOS. Thanks to @tovi for the suggestion.
12. Fixed slicing failures occurring with some files.
13. Thanks for the translation update #369 #330
